| | Friend - The June 9 Georgia General Primary is coming up fast, but there's still time to request your absentee ballot and vote by mail! Voting by mail is the safest and easiest way to make your voice heard, and it helps ensure that voters with disabilities, language barriers, and others who need to vote in person are able to do so safely. Questions about voting by mail? [Find out how to request a ballot along with answers to many of the most common vote by mail questions at GAVBM.com. ](https://secure.fairfightaction.com/page/m/41efa413/34a064cc/1bb0743a/7029e0f/2763644582/VEsE/?g=yzW-IFH8x8VbBlZb5iRRXGA "http://gavbm.com/")If you've already received your ballot request or ballot, make sure to complete it and return it as soon as possible! name: Holcomb Crew
subject: Great government service continues
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Eric Holcomb for Indiana
body:
From Day One, Governor Holcomb has made providing great government service one of his pillars in leading Indiana – and that commitment continues today | | |
---|---|---
Friends,

There’s no doubt that some things have changed over the last few months – but one thing remains constant: **Governor Holcomb and your state’s commitment to great government service for ALL Hoosiers.**

And that means ALL Hoosiers...including our most vulnerable, our motorists, and anyone needing emergency medical help.

**_Department of Child Services_**

The Indiana Department of Child Services is continuing its work on the frontlines advancing its critical mission of protecting Indiana’s at-risk children.

While some work has moved virtual – if there’s an allegation of child abuse or neglect, a family case manager is visiting in-person to investigate.

But that’s where we need your help. **If you suspect a child is being harmed, please place a call at anytime to the state’s Child Abuse & Neglect Hotline at 1-800-800-5556.** As schools and many community activities have closed, calls to the hotline have dropped by more than 40% -- but sadly, children are still at risk.

_**Bureau of Motor Vehicles**_

The Indiana Bureau of Motor Vehicles is now open by appointment and is ready to help you with important services – like knowledge testing, a new vehicle registration, a new driver’s license, a replacement license and more.

**Just last week, the BMV served Hoosiers with 141,854 appointments, and they’re adding an additional 2,000 appointments a day to better serve you.**

**[Check here](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=02c5ed20fedb487c89f47b6cfe4a4de2&_e=ImaLquwe4zoJF9U0_x8IQO2wIiPe3rAXGUvsAFmhKq5cf3x3fO3SWdygKFnQsUEdOwNY0mgbdVHEMgnXqYPbYgUF3DVvCmBfMGdImVglaOLqU6yaKEQiMGDrYgEL1IsdtusaPbYeMFfkEuT1t-bADIOsbu0si-MzokqtmtcPpXKFUEoIzTnMesYDxF4mqTdAij4ri4TgFJ8OngXQunkRI_kfUIfL6Er21CMsFJyVvMxckz4DL3ClJstTs1cyFeq6AS_r_O0sk4sF3I48qxfeTzwA0QUMWzsQdaEWee0aJ3xw-0q_508RgAqhy8qP_oRVX-2_8-pEdGfkcYybqjJcKAQ-hCyAY8fYz29gVQWyRutd9UA5ctQ628J2npG5NNQp)** to make an appointment, and to see if you can complete your transaction online. And remember – Governor Holcomb has ordered an extension on all license and registration expiration dates through June 4.

_**Emergency Medical Services**_

This is National EMS Week – and a great reminder to thank those medical professionals who are ALWAYS on the frontlines serving us!

**From Day One, Governor Holcomb has made providing great government service one of his pillars in leading Indiana – and that commitment continues today.**

#INThisTogether,
-Your Holcomb Crew
---
---
|
|
| |
---

|

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: Washington Post admits, 'Not enough people to test!'
body:
Today’s Washington Post front page might confuse some readers: “As coronavirus testing expands, a new problem arises: Not enough people to test.”

https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=1363664a7f&e=3ee4985eea
The White House • May 18, 2020

** ‘Not enough people to test!’
------------------------------------------------------------
Today’s Washington Post front page might confuse some readers: “As coronavirus testing expands, a new problem arises: Not enough people to test.”

That’s odd. After months of claiming that America lagged the world in testing capacity, the partisan media never corrected itself. It just changed the story and moved on.

🎬 WATCH: America’s testing capacity is unmatched and unrivaled! (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=99cf618996&e=3ee4985eea)

Here are the facts the media left out. Under President Trump, the United States has built the strongest Coronavirus testing system in the world. In addition to performing more tests than any other country on Earth—by far—America has now conducted twice as many tests per capita as South Korea, long considered a gold standard for testing.

On every supply issue, from ventilators to testing to the availability of food (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=5494af3136&e=3ee4985eea) , President Trump has mobilized a massive response across government and the private sector. As a result, many areas today see their testing capacity exceed demand—exactly what happened with ventilators (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=549f2ddbd3&e=3ee4985eea) after President Trump drastically expanded production.

Being overprepared is a good “problem” to have, and it didn’t happen by accident. Thanks to the hard work of President Trump, our public health experts, and private sector leaders, America is more ready than ever for a safe, phased reopening.

See President Trump’s tweet on The Washington Post’s front page. (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8ae8ec489a&e=3ee4985eea)

By the numbers: America leads the world in testing! (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=6a83136bb5&e=3ee4985eea)

** 🚀 WATCH: Space Force flag unveiled!
------------------------------------------------------------
More than 70 years ago, the U.S. military added the Air Force as its fifth branch. On Friday, President Trump participated as the next great flag in our Armed Forces was revealed in the Oval Office.

🎬 America has a new military flag to wave proudly! (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8fa5d33985&e=3ee4985eea)

The U.S. Space Force is the sixth branch of our military, established in December as part of President Trump’s vision for rebuilding American defense. For decades, the United States led the world in space. But in recent years, our adversaries have sought to weaponize this “final frontier,” hoping that America wouldn’t rise to meet the challenge.

But we did. “The importance strategically, militarily, and even from a pure civilian standpoint, and from bringing our economy back—everything—it’s going to help so much,” President Trump said on Friday.

“This is a very special moment.”

IN PHOTOS: Space Force flag is presented in the Oval Office (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=d105c5f6bc&e=3ee4985eea)

** Photo of the Day
------------------------------------------------------------
President Trump, joined by Defense Secretary Mark Esper and CMSgt Roger Towberman of the US Space Force, signs an Armed Forces Day Proclamation | May 15, 2020

Privacy Policy (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8825631c34&e=3ee4985eea) | Contact the White House (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=83fde44d13&e=3ee4985eea) | Unsubscribe (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=e18f6f4698)

============================================================

After months of claiming that America lagged the world in testing capacity, the partisan media never corrected itself. It just changed the story and moved on. 🎬 WATCH: America’s testing capacity is unmatched and unrivaled! () Here are the facts the media left out. Under President Trump, the United States has built the strongest Coronavirus testing system in the world. In addition to performing more tests than any other country on Earth—by far—America has now conducted twice as many tests per capita as South Korea, long considered a gold standard for testing. On every supply issue, from ventilators to testing to the availability of food () , President Trump has mobilized a massive response across government and the private sector. As a result, many areas today see their testing capacity exceed demand—exactly what happened with ventilators () after President Trump drastically expanded production. Being overprepared is a good “problem” to have, and it didn’t happen by accident. Thanks to the hard work of President Trump, our public health experts, and private sector leaders, America is more ready than ever for a safe, phased reopening. See President Trump’s tweet on The Washington Post’s front page. () By the numbers: America leads the world in testing! () 🚀 WATCH: Space Force flag unveiled! More than 70 years ago, the U.S. military added the Air Force as its fifth branch. On Friday, President Trump participated as the next great flag in our Armed Forces was revealed in the Oval Office. 🎬 America has a new military flag to wave proudly! () The U.S. Space Force is the sixth branch of our military, established in December as part of President Trump’s vision for rebuilding American defense. For decades, the United States led the world in space. But in recent years, our adversaries have sought to weaponize this “final frontier,” hoping that America wouldn’t rise to meet the challenge. But we did. “The importance strategically, militarily, and even from a pure civilian standpoint, and from bringing our economy back—everything—it’s going to help so much,” President Trump said on Friday. “This is a very special moment.” IN PHOTOS: Space Force flag is presented in the Oval Office () ** Photo of the Day President Trump, joined by Defense Secretary Mark Esper and CMSgt Roger Towberman of the US Space Force, signs an Armed Forces Day Proclamation | May 15, 2020
